channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Tinubu’s crave for commendation amidst awful performance
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Propaganda is the systematic dissemination of information, including facts, half-truths, rumours, or lies, designed to influence public opinion, shape behaviours, or promote a specific political cause or agenda. It typically uses emotional appeal rather than logical evidence, often suppressing oppos

item.title: I heard students shouting ‘bandits, bandits’: Oriire survivor narrates dramatic capture
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Tales of students being abducted from schools have become all too common in Nigeria, but until recently, nothing of the sort had ever been recorded in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State. The post I heard students shouting ‘bandits, bandits’: Oriire survivor narrates dramatic cap

item.title: How to avoid Tumbu fly attack on skin
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The average person in Nigeria has “committed” the “offence” of hanging washed clothes outside with the inside surface exposed to dry under the sun. But doing this seemingly innocuous practice, which many people indulge in, has put them at the risk of getting infested with Tumbu fly larva. The post H

item.title: Doc, I think I am obese
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: I sent back a teaser to her. “Are you overweight or obese?”. She replied with a different version. “Doc, I think I have excess weight. I then understood her dilemma. She was obese. Most women do not want to be called fat or obese. They use indeterminate language to describe their excess weight gain.

item.title: Is the NYSC reform another uniform makeover?
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: In a country where “national service” has too often translated into national sacrifice, the Federal Government has rolled out what it calls the most comprehensive overhaul of the National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) in its 53-year history. Approved by the Federal Executive Council (FEC), the reforms

item.title: Still on urgent need to curb road traffic crashes in Nigeria
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The rising rate of road traffic crashes in Nigeria has become a national emergency that demands immediate and decisive action. Hardly a week passes without reports of devastating crashes claiming dozens of lives and leaving many families in mourning. These tragedies are not mere statistics. They rep

item.title: New EU border system tripling time at passport control, airport boss says
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Ryanair has also warned passengers travelling to Europe this summer to prepare for extended waits.

item.title: Peter Obi: Integrity as currency
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Peter Obi, the Presidential standard bearer of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), is a rare breed. Sometimes, you just wonder: Is he truly a Nigerian? How can a man of means, influence and affluence be so humble, ordinary, unassuming and ruthlessly obsequious? This is not about his politics. Or

item.title: Sources of insecurity in Nigeria
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Truly, I had told myself we have done enough on insecurity on this page and therefore should leave that for a while. As it turned out the decision didn't last because of a very important question Professor Chidi Odinkalu pushed through at the security summit organised on the penultimate week by This

item.title: Tate brothers arrested in US after more charges laid against them in UK
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The Crown Prosecution Service said it would seek to extradite the brothers from the US, while their lawyer dismissed the new charges as "slander".

item.title: Time to protect the other woman
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: It has become very important to discuss this controversial issue, which concerns married men who choose to keep ‘other women’ in their lives for whatever reason. The moment a man separates, divorces, is widowed or even becomes polygamous by choice, he should protect the other woman. Let me say it ag

item.title: Cultists trailing rivals linked to NURTW leaders open fire on police
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: We did not know there were policemen in the area. It was Temidayo who fired the gun, while I was hit by bullets fired by the policemen.” The post Cultists trailing rivals linked to NURTW leaders open fire on police appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Broken, yet not broken
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Becky and Emeka shared a deep bond that had lasted for eight years. They had built a life together filled with dreams of marriage, laughter, and love. Friends often said they were perfect for each other. However, everything changed one afternoon when a secret from Becky’s past surfaced and shattered

item.title: FIFA admits hydration breaks may not continue after World Cup criticism
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: FIFA will analyse the impact of hydration breaks after the World Cup, says Arsene Wenger a day before the final.

item.title: What FG must do to stop kidnapping, revive economy –Ayorinde
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The General Overseer/Founder of the World Evangelism Bible Church (WEBIC), Prophet Samson Ayorinde, has listed a more collaboration among the security agencies as the way forward for the country to stop the kidnapping menace across the country. The post What FG must do to stop kidnapping, revive eco

item.title: My battle with cervical cancer, ailing actress, Abiola Ayomide speaks
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Ailing actress, Abiola Ayomide, has opened up about her battle with cervical cancer, sharing an emotional account of the health challenge that changed her life and inspired her to speak publicly in the hope of encouraging other women to prioritise their health. The post My battle with cervical cance

item.title: After Orire, Kogi operation: 529 kidnapped schoolchildren, worshipers, farmers, others remain in captivity
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Barely days after security forces secured the release of the 39 pupils and seven teachers abducted from Oriire, Oyo State, another school came under attack. item.title: Argentina fans flood NYC’s Times Square before World Cup final
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Hundreds of Argentina fans packed New York City’s Times Square hours before Sunday's World Cup final.

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THE Yaba Lions Club, District 404A3, has called for urgent and sustained efforts to eradicate drug abuse among young people, describing the growing trend as a major threat to societal and mental well-being. item.title: Venezuela’s Official Earthquake Death Toll Rises to 5,119
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The official death toll from the twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela last month rose to 5,119, according to National Assembly head Jorge Rodríguez.

item.title: Gunmen kidnap three mourning siblings, seize mother’s corpse in Benue
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Three siblings travelling from Abuja to Benue State with the remains of their late mother have reportedly been abducted by suspected armed herdsmen along the Ogobia–Otukpo Road in Ohimini Local Government Area of the state. item.title: England beat France in 10-goal thriller to win third-place play-off
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Bukayo Saka scores a hat-trick as England beat France 6-4 in a thriller to record their best men's World Cup finish since 1966.

item.description: Kylian Mbappé made history at the World Cup by becoming the tournament's all-time leading goalscorer with 22 goals after his brace in France's third-place playoff against England. The French superstar surpassed Lionel Messi's previous record of 21 goals, cementing his legacy as one of football's gre

item.title: Bellingham breaks England World Cup goals record
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Jude Bellingham becomes the first Englishman to score seven goals at a single World Cup with a late strike against France.

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Titilayo Obileye shares her inspiring journey from journalism to strategic government communications, reflecting on nearly a decade of public service. In this interview with Yetunde Ajanaku, she discusses governance and the importance of trust and what it takes to build a lasting career. The post I’

item.dc:creator: France 24

item.title: England's best World Cup for 60 years - but how should it be judged?
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England have secured their best men's World Cup finish since winning the tournament 60 years ago. Is their performance at the 2026 edition being under-appreciated?

item.title: Two US troops killed and one missing after Iranian attack in Jordan
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Iran and the US have traded strikes as hostilities re-escalated this week, with Tehran targeting US military facilities across the Gulf region.

item.title: Hat-trick hero Saka: I'm fit and wanted to play more
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Bukayo Saka revealed he "wanted to play more" at the World Cup after scoring a hat-trick to earn England a bronze medal against France - after Thomas Tuchel tactically left him out of the semi-final loss to Argentina.

item.title: England claim third at World Cup with win in 10-goal thriller over France
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Bukayo Saka’s hat-trick helped England to a remarkable 6-4 bronze medal win over France in Miami to achieve their best World Cup finish since 1966.

item.description: Russians have been hit by mobile internet shutdowns and more businesses are seeking to dodge tax after more than four years of war with Ukraine.

item.title: How did Sheriff happen to Delta?
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Ejiro Idama Delta people are resilient. We will manage flood, manage NEPA, abi BEDC sef, manage fuel price, and still find time to manage politics. But every once in a while, a question escapes from the beer parlour, the keke, the WhatsApp group: “How did Sheriff happen to Delta State?” It’s not

item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Kylian Mbappe leads the Golden Boot race by two goals but Lionel Messi could yet overtake him in the World Cup final.

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: ART has often taken inspiration from other fields, showing ideas that science can measure but not always show. Babatunde Atolagbe’s recent photos sit at this crossroads, using portrait techniques The post What the portrait doesn’t say… appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: The odd quirks of Peter Obi at sixty-five
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Valentine Obienyem The Greek biographer Plutarch, in the preamble to his ”Life of Alexander”, observed: ”I am writing biography, not history; and the most brilliant exploits do not always manifest a man’s virtue or vice so well as a slight thing, a word or a jest, which often tells us more of his

item.title: Saka Scores Hat-trick As England Edge Out France In 10-Goal Thriller, Clinch First-Ever World Cup Bronze
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: A hat-trick from Bukayo Saka helped England edge out France 6-4, in a thrilling FIFA World Cup 2026 third-place play-off on Saturday. The win means England clinched their first-ever bronze medal at a FIFA World Cup, having finished fourth in 1990 and 2018. France captain Kylian scored twice which to

item.title: Mbappé Becomes World Cup’s All-Time Top Scorer
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: France forward Kylian Mbappé has become the highest goalscorer in the history of the FIFA World Cup after taking his tally to 22 goals. The record-breaking feat saw Mbappé become the leading scorer in the men’s World Cup, surpassing the previous all-time mark. The French captain also extended his le

item.description: England secured third place at the 2026 FIFA World Cup after edging France 6-4 in a pulsating ten-goal contest at Miami Stadium on Saturday, avenging their heartbreak from Wednesday’s semi-final defeat to Argentina with a share of the consolation prize. item.description: England claimed the bronze medal at the 2026 FIFA World Cup after surviving a dramatic French comeback to record a pulsating 6-4 victory in an unforgettable third-place play-off, with Bukayo Saka producing a magnificent hat-trick. The Three Lions appeared to have the contest wrapped up by half-time

item.title: Saka nets hat-trick as England beat France in 10-goal thriller to secure World Cup Bronze
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Saka then took centre stage. The Arsenal winger struck twice before the break, first finishing Marcus Rashford's incisive pass in the 37th minute before firing home from the edge of the area in first-half stoppage time to send England into the interval with a commanding 4-0 lead.

item.title: England beat France 6-4 in World Cup third-place playoff
item.dc:creator: Vanguard Sports

item.title: Moving without arriving: Oluyemi’s rat race series

item.description: Pelumi Oluyemi often turns everyday experiences into metaphors for bigger social issues. She uses familiar places to explore themes like work, ambition, and daily routines. The post Moving without arriving: Oluyemi’s rat race series appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: What Are No Deposit Bonus Codes and How Can You Get Them?
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Before activating any no deposit bonus, it is important to understand the accompanying terms and conditions. Wagering requirements remain one of the most significant factors, as they determine how much betting must be completed before bonus winnings become eligible for withdrawal.

item.description: Luke Littler fired in an average of over 109 to kick off his World Matchplay defence in empathic fashion on Saturday.

item.description: The Presidency has alleged that the presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Peter Obi, has adopted a new political strategy ahead of The post 2027: Presidency reveals Peter Obi’s latest political strategy appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Real-Time LuaTeX: Recompiling Large Documents in 1ms [pdf]

item.title: ICPC Secures Final Forfeiture of N941.9m Traced to IPPIS Payroll Scam
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC) has secured a final court order forfeiting ₦941,994,079.86 linked to an alleged Integrated Payroll and Personnel Information System (IPPIS) payroll fraud involving suspected ghost workers to the Federal Government. The co

item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://uncovered.ink Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48962893 Points: 43 # Comments: 31

item.title: Stakeholders call for stronger investment in parenting support to protect children, strengthen families
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The call was made during a stakeholder dissemination workshop titled "From the Airwaves to Stronger Families: Evaluating Parenting 101 with Isang and Shaping the Future of Parenting Support in Akwa Ibom State," The post Stakeholders call for stronger investment in parenting support to protect childr

item.title: Troops Dismantle Suspected Illegal Bunkering Site, Arrest 6 In Lagos
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Troops of the Nigerian Army’s 65 Battalion have dismantled a suspected illegal bunkering site in the Orile General Area of Lagos State, arresting six suspects and recovering vehicles and petroleum products believed to be linked to the illicit operation. The operation, carried out on July 16, f

item.title: Lagos bunkering site dismantled as army seizes vehicles, petroleum products
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “The suspects and recovered items were subsequently handed over to the Nigeria Security and Civil Defence Corps (NSCDC) Lagos State Command by the Commanding Officer, 65 Battalion, Lieutenant Colonel S Adeojo, for further investigation and necessary legal action.”

item.title: Police Nab Fake Pastor, Accomplice Over Abduction Of 4-Week-Old Baby In Niger
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Niger State Police Command has arrested a suspected fake pastor and his alleged accomplice for the abduction of a four-week-old baby in Minna, the state capital. The Command’s Public Relations Officer, SP Wasiu Abiodun, disclosed this in a statement issued on Saturday, saying the baby has

item.title: Nigerian Rocket Ajayi blasts 9.84s to stun London Diamond League
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The Auburn University product and Nigerian record holder clocked a wind-assisted but legal 9.84 seconds (-0.7 m/s) to edge out Jamaica’s reigning world champion Oblique Seville, who finished second in 9.87 seconds.

item.dc:creator: Hacker News

item.title: Northants beat Hampshire in thriller to win first Vitality Blast title in a decade
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: ​​​​​​​Northamptonshire claimed their first Vitality Blast title in a decade and third overall with a thrilling 14-run win over Hampshire in Saturday's see-saw Edgbaston final.

item.title: 2 US Troops Killed, 1 Missing After Iranian Missile, Drone Attack In Jordan
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Two United States service members have been killed and another declared missing following an Iranian missile and drone attack on a military installation in Jordan, the US Central Command (CENTCOM) confirmed on Saturday. The incident marks the first American military fatalities attributed to Iranian
